text: He's one of a small but growing number of Canadian cancer patients who are having their lung cancer removed not through the usual open chest incision, rather through small holes just a few centimeters wide.
I do think it is the wave of the future.
In standard lung surgery, the ribs have to be spread 8 to 12 inches with a metal expander, as shown here in this hospital video.
They then remove part of the lung that contains the tumor and put the tissue in a plastic bag.
